title: Making a Hyperpop Song on the SP-404 MK2
subtitle: MUS-47H · A 3-week genre production intensive
description: Write, produce, and finish a complete hyperpop song entirely on the Roland SP-404 MK2. A genre production course that starts with where the sound came from (PC Music, SOPHIE, 100 gecs), builds its palette on the sampler (hard clipped drums, a distorted 808, supersaw-style synths from sampled notes), then puts your own voice at the center: writing a hook, recording it into the SP, and processing it with Auto Pitch, formant shift, pitch-up doubles, and stacked harmonies, before vocal chops, ear candy, a real pop arrangement, and a loud bright mix finish the song. Taught through daily lectures, reference listening, machine time, quizzes, and three projects that ladder into the final track. Builds on MUS-45H, the beatmaking course.

lessons.title: Field Guide: The Hyperpop Sound on the SP
Days 1–7: The Genre, the Machine, Drums, the 808, Synths, Your First Loop
Days 8–14: The Hook, Recording, Auto Pitch, Stacks, Vocal Chops, Ear Candy, the Drop
Days 15–21: Pop Structure, Builds, Transitions, Dynamics, the Loud Mix, Your Finished Song
